reasoning:
The current target rate is 3.50%. The FOMC is currently in an easing or paused macro environment, having reduced rates from 4.25% to 3.50% by December 2025 and holding steady at the January 27-28, 2026 meeting. 

The question asks whether the lower limit of the federal funds target range will have *increased* by 2026-02-22 and 2026-03-17 compared to the 3.50% baseline on 2026-02-15. 

Looking at the upcoming FOMC schedule, there are no scheduled meetings between Feb 15 and March 16. The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for March 17-18, 2026. The rate decision and statement will be announced on the second day (Wednesday, March 18) at 2:00 PM EST, meaning any scheduled target range adjustments would not take effect or be published for the dates in question (the value applicable to March 17 will still be the old 3.50% set in January). 

Therefore, for the rate to have *increased* by either February 22 or March 17, the Fed would have to execute an emergency, inter-meeting rate *hike*. Given that the Fed just executed multiple 25bps rate cuts in late 2025 and recently held steady, an inter-meeting hike over the next month is tremendously improbable. P(increase) is capped at the 0.05 minimum allowed floor for the first date, with a slightly higher baseline for the second date purely to account for the mechanically expanded window for any emergency policy action and to follow the requirement that longer horizons have probabilities closer to 0.5.
